name: Austria-Hungary (1869–1918)

colors.symbolism: details of the coats of arms

description: A horizontally divided dual flag — the upper half black-yellow (Austrian Imperial colours) and the lower half red-white-green (Hungarian colours) — with the coats of arms of Austria and Hungary placed centrally on each half.
history: The dual monarchy of Austria-Hungary was created by the Compromise of 1867. The 'common' flag combining the colours of both halves, with the two coats of arms, was used by joint Austro-Hungarian institutions (notably the Imperial and Royal armed forces — the k.u.k. Kriegsmarine) until the empire's dissolution in 1918.

features.symbolism: Austrian black-yellow above, Hungarian red-white-green below
